description: §31 E-DG-002 POSITIVE — a reactive cell declared but NEVER consumed draws E-DG-002 at severity Warning. `<orphan> = "never read"` is declared at file scope (V5-strict structural form) and read by NOTHING (no `${@orphan}`, no logic read, no `^{}` meta read) → `readers.size === 0` → the dependency-graph pass fires E-DG-002 ("declared but never consumed"). This POS became authorable at S260 once the §34 row was corrected to match the impl + §22.6: the S78-backfill row had said severity Error + a near-opposite "read in a context whose wiring can't be established" trigger; the impl (dependency-graph.ts:3191) has always emitted `"warning"` on `readers.size === 0`, and §22.6 (SPEC.md:15863-15866) presupposes the consumption-keyed trigger. Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 ignored by superset matching. Paired with the landed dg-002-no-readers-neg (a consumed cell stays silent).
rationale: §31.2: the graph represents all reactive dependencies — every expression that reads a `@variable` is a dependent edge. A cell with zero such edges is unconsumed. §22.6 (SPEC.md:15863-15866) normatively fixes E-DG-002 as consumption-keyed (a read inside a `^{}` meta block counts as consumed; a cell read nowhere is not). Impl: dependency-graph.ts:3184 `if (readers.size === 0)` → `new DGError("E-DG-002", …, "warning")` at :3191. The S260 §34-vs-impl audit + user ruling corrected the catalog row (severity Error→Warning, trigger rewritten to never-consumed) so this assertion is now SPEC-true, not impl-enshrining.
